name: Teleprompter

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Added support for callouts in the "Markdown" view mode.
- Changed the icon of the "Plain text" view mode.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# New commands
Added **12** new commands for accessibility and automation purposes. Ten of these commands allow you to choose the font family, and two are for selecting the current view mode. Here are the details:
## Font family
- Default (ID: control:content:select:Default)  
- Arial (ID: control:content:select:Arial)  
- Courier New (ID: control:content:select:Courier_New)  
- Georgia (ID: control:content:select:Georgia)  
- Helvetica (ID: control:content:select:Helvetica)  
- Times New Roman (ID: control:content:select:Times_New_Roman)  
- Trebuchet MS (ID: control:content:select:Trebuchet_MS)  
- Tahoma (ID: control:content:select:Tahoma)  
- Roboto (ID: control:content:select:Roboto)  
- Verdana (ID: control:content:select:Verdana)

## View mode
- Markdown (ID: control:content:select:content-md)  
- Plain text (ID: control:content:select:content-plain)

# Renamed URL action
The Obsidian protocol handler action has been renamed from "teleprompter:open" to "teleprompter-open" to ensure compatibility with more API clients. This change was necessary after discovering issues with the original URL format in the Stream Deck app.

Example URL:
```
obsidian://teleprompter-open?content=test&placement=window&pin=yes&countdown=5&play=yes 
```

# Fixes
- Full-screen mode activation no longer faults when triggered by a commands while the Obsidian app is not in focus.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# New features

## API
Added a public API to allow programmatic control over key plugin features. The API is accessible through the `api` object found within the plugin's instance in Obsidian, specifically at `app.plugins.plugins.teleprompter`.

The API currently offers two methods:
- `open(params)`
- `close()`

For complete API usage details, see the README file in the project root. Documentation may shift to a separate file as the API evolves.

Resolves #19.

![api-open](https://i.imgur.com/JBQHAHq.gif)

## URI launcher
The new URI handler enables opening the teleprompter with specific files or content using the `obsidian://teleprompter:open` URI along with additional parameters, mirroring the `open` method's parameters in the API.
  
Example:
```  
obsidian://teleprompter:open?file=Folder/My%20Note.md&placement=window&pin=yes&countdown=5&play=yes 
```

This URL does the following:
- `file=Folder/My%20Note.md`: Opens the teleprompter with the specified file.
- `pin=yes`: Pins the content to prevent updates from other interactions.
- `countdown=5`: Initiates a 5-second countdown.
- `play=yes`: Starts the teleprompter after the countdown.

## Additional commands
Added seven new commands:

- Open file in sidebar and pin it (ID: open-app:sidebar:file)  
- Open file in a new tab and pin it (ID: open-app:new-tab:file)  
- Open file in a new window and pin it (ID: open-app:new-window:file)

![open-file-in-new-window-and-pin](https://i.imgur.com/YxOIpGO.gif)

- Open arbitrary text in sidebar and pin it (ID: open-app:sidebar:text)  
- Open arbitrary text in a new tab and pin it (ID: open-app:new-tab:text)  
- Open arbitrary text in a new window and pin it (ID: open-app:new-window:text)

![open-text-in-new-window-and-pin](https://i.imgur.com/M1msSYI.gif)

- Close teleprompter (ID: close)

## Note properties display
Teleprompter now adheres to the settings in Obsidian under "Settings" -> "Editor" -> "Properties in document". Note properties will be hidden in the teleprompter window if the option is set to "Hidden". Resolves #14.

![editor-properties](https://imgur.com/c4DjlUl.png)


# Bug fixes
- Fixed the issue where the countdown control icon would disappear when the countdown value was entered using the input box instead of the range slider.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# New features

## Countdown

Introducing Countdown control, a programmable timer that delays automatic scrolling of your teleprompter content after hitting "Play", giving you better control over your presentation pacing.

Resolves #9.

How it looks:

![countdown-demo](https://i.imgur.com/BnTu2PF.gif)

How its settings look:

![countdown-settings](https://github.com/lumetrium/obsidian-teleprompter/assets/125469344/7b2fa886-48af-4813-ac95-00f637b11a32)

## Eyeline indicator panel: background color customization

Eyeline indicator panel has been updated to support background color customization. Choose from three options to match your style:

- **Default:** The standard look you know and love.
- **Follow content:** Automatically matches the background color of your content for a cohesive visual experience.
- **Custom:** Pick any color that suits your taste.

How it looks:
![eyeline-indicator-settings](https://github.com/lumetrium/obsidian-teleprompter/assets/125469344/6f670e6a-642b-404c-a63a-93c2024f6734)


# Minor changes

## Default Eyeline indicator panel

The Eyeline indicator has been promoted to a default panel, simplifying access for both new and existing users. This change means it will automatically be included for new users upon installation and for existing users through selecting "Restore defaults" in the panels settings.

This is now the default:
![default-panels](https://github.com/lumetrium/obsidian-teleprompter/assets/125469344/bd50dd7f-efdf-43df-a53f-f6d1f444eabe)


# Bug fixes
- Fixed the issue where line breaks were not properly displayed in the teleprompter's content, resolves #10.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# New features

## Eyeline indicator

Introducing Eyeline indicator, a dedicated panel that features a stationary arrow to guide your eyes as you read through text. This visual cue assists in maintaining focus on a specific line, facilitating a consistent reading flow. It's draggable and remains fixed on the screen wherever you position it.  You can also adjust its color in settings. 

Resolves #3. 

How it looks:
![eyeline-indicator](https://github.com/lumetrium/obsidian-teleprompter/assets/125469344/13989507-5b3d-464f-bc5a-cca141e439d9)

How its settings look:
![eyeline-settings](https://github.com/lumetrium/obsidian-teleprompter/assets/125469344/cc2513af-19e7-4b82-b736-78c68643c4f5)

# Bug fixes
- Fixed the issue when turning off "Detach window" option within the main Obsidian settings panel.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Minor improvements

## External note changes

Teleprompter now picks up external changes made to notes. This addresses issue #2, where changes made to notes on one device were not reflected in the teleprompter on another when using software like Syncthing.

## Improved mobile settings interface

The settings interface has been optimized for smaller screens, ensuring a smooth experience on smartphones and narrow displays.

# Bug fixes

- Resolved color picker issues when using teleprompter in a separate window.
- Fixed Obsidian mobile app freezing when closing the teleprompter view while in full-screen mode.
- Eliminated an extra empty line that would occasionally appear when using the teleprompter in a separate window.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# New features

## Full-screen control 
Maximize the teleprompter view with a single click of a button or using a hotkey. A big thanks to Reddit users u/Dwev and u/ademenchuk for [suggesting](https://www.reddit.com/r/ObsidianMD/comments/1794jm8/im_developing_a_teleprompter_plugin_for_obsidian/?utm_source=share&utm_medium=web2x&context=3) this feature!

![378__31 10 2023_14 56 27__Obsidian](https://github.com/lumetrium/obsidian-teleprompter/assets/125469344/515f4323-949d-4fcb-8297-741a0cd969cf)

## Detach window control (desktop only)
Desktop users can now easily open the teleprompter in a separate window. While it's still possible to drag the tab with the plugin to create a separate window, this method is not particularly intuitive or user-friendly. Therefore, there's now a dedicated control. Special thanks to [ericvida from the Obsidian forum](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/teleprompter-window/14178) for suggesting this feature!

![379__31 10 2023_14 57 13__Obsidian](https://github.com/lumetrium/obsidian-teleprompter/assets/125469344/4cea970a-866e-47bf-9706-596bf7a839c1)

## Opacity control (desktop only)
Customize the teleprompter window's transparency with the new opacity control. This feature is perfect for ensuring the teleprompter's content remains visible while not completely blocking the view of the window behind it.

![380__31 10 2023_15 18 27__Obsidian](https://github.com/lumetrium/obsidian-teleprompter/assets/125469344/8b0bea8a-6f48-42c5-9748-b148e70b3383)

## Keep awake for mobile
Say goodbye to screen timeouts on your mobile device! With the new "Keep awake" feature, your screen will stay active and won't go to sleep while the teleprompter is open. Thanks to Reddit user u/derkork for [bringing up](https://www.reddit.com/r/ObsidianMD/comments/1794jm8/comment/k6fxe0x/?context=3) this issue!

## Hotkeys for opening teleprompter in specific locations
Three new commands were added: "Open in sidebar", "Open in new tab", and "Open in new window". You can assign hotkeys to them to quickly open the teleprompter in your desired location. This resolves the good first issue https://github.com/lumetrium/obsidian-teleprompter/issues/1.

![386__31 10 2023_18 23 06__Obsidian](https://github.com/lumetrium/obsidian-teleprompter/assets/125469344/a3906df3-b382-4367-a060-9c9ac43869a4)

# User experience
In the "Controls" tab of the settings, each control now includes a short and informative description, making it easier to understand their purpose and how to use them effectively.

![384__31 10 2023_18 22 16__Obsidian](https://github.com/lumetrium/obsidian-teleprompter/assets/125469344/8258540d-436b-4352-a645-297571cd7097)
